title: What clients usually want to know first.
kicker: If your question isn't here, the fastest answer is a 30-minute call.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: A 30-minute scoping call, a one-page diagnostic the next week, and an agreed scorecard before any spend moves. From there: weekly working sessions and a monthly review against the scorecard. No retainer is signed before we agree on what success looks like.

items.q: Do you work in-house or as an external consultant?
items.a: Both, and at the moment, both at once. I lead marketing in-house at Opal Hotel Amman and run a small number of paid-media advisories through New Aeon Digital alongside it. Engagements are sized to fit; I don't take work I can't show up for properly.

items.q: Which markets and sectors do you work in?
items.a: Mostly Jordan, KSA, the UAE and Kuwait — Arabic-first when it matters, English when it's the working language. Sectors I know best: hospitality, ecommerce, and B2B professional services. I won't take a brief in a sector I haven't operated in before.

On the same metric your CFO uses. RevPAR for hotels, ROAS and contribution margin for ecommerce, qualified pipeline and sales-cycle length for B2B. Reporting is reconciled weekly against the source-of-truth system (PMS, Shopify, CRM) — not against the ad platform's optimistic numbers.

items.q: Will you train my in-house team?
items.a: Yes — and that's usually the goal. As a Meta Lead Trainer and Google Premier Partner, I install the operating system (naming, cadence, dashboards) and hand it off. Six-month handoff plans with measurable exit criteria are the default.

items.q: What's the smallest engagement you take on?
items.a: A two-week paid-media diagnostic for a single account or property. No commitment beyond that. If we both want to keep going, we scope a longer engagement on real evidence rather than on a sales pitch.
